author: Chestnut
text:
§9 create emits the minted id to stdout (+ verb result lines)

After a successful seal, mutate::run now emits the op result instead of returning Ok(()) silently. create prints the minted id ALONE to stdout — the sole machine channel, so id=$(bl create …) captures it clean; claim/unclaim/update/close/drop print a terse confirmation to stderr.

The id is read back from the sealed commit's bl-id trailer, not the pre-seal IdScheme mint, so a create/pre id reassignment is reflected (§5). Emission lifted to src/mutate_report.rs to hold mutate.rs under the 300-line cap.
